SPEC 7: update for NumPy 2.2.0","user":{"login":"mdhaber","id":6570539,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjY1NzA1Mzk=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/6570539?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber","html_url":"https://github.com/mdhaber","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"numpy/numpy#27771 allows `default_rng` to accept instances of `RandomState` and convert them to a `Generator`. This updates the SPEC accordingly.

*Update: before merge, also correct single-backtick `Generator` in suggested `rng` documentation:*

> Types other than `numpy.random.Generator` are passed to `numpy.random.default_rng` to instantiate a `Generator`.

*This looks fine in markdown, but in ReST it needs two backticks.*

---

The first stage of SciPy's SPEC 7 transition is essentially complete:
https://github.com/scipy/scipy/issues/21833

For standard cases, other libraries might be interested in how we extended the decorator to inject standard documentation about the transition, e.g.

![{EB7EB439-218F-4660-8B30-EF4BDE09EB49}](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0d83245a-8cb2-46b8-9caf-9968b8f42ec6)

We also added standard tests:
https://github.com/scipy/scipy/blob/82ee02556872b9f91576b835a8f40f1d9eb1fd8d/scipy/_lib/tests/test__util.py#L556

It also might be useful to see in how we handled some of the oddball cases, like `seed` arguments that already converted integers to `Generators` (instead of `RandomState`), existing `rng` arguments that worked more like most legacy `seed`/`random_state` arguments, and `random_state` attributes of classes, but I'm not sure if all that can be summarized.

2.2.0","user":{"login":"mdhaber","id":6570539,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjY1NzA1Mzk=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/6570539?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber","html_url":"https://github.com/mdhaber","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"numpy/numpy#27771 allows `default_rng` to accept instances of `RandomState` and convert them to a `Generator`. This updates the SPEC accordingly.

*Update: before merge, also correct single-backtick `Generator` in suggested `rng` documentation:*

> Types other than `numpy.random.Generator` are passed to `numpy.random.default_rng` to instantiate a `Generator`.

*This looks fine in markdown, but in ReST it needs two backticks.*

---

The first stage of SciPy's SPEC 7 transition is essentially complete:
https://github.com/scipy/scipy/issues/21833

For standard cases, other libraries might be interested in how we extended the decorator to inject standard documentation about the transition, e.g.

![{EB7EB439-218F-4660-8B30-EF4BDE09EB49}](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0d83245a-8cb2-46b8-9caf-9968b8f42ec6)

We also added standard tests:
https://github.com/scipy/scipy/blob/82ee02556872b9f91576b835a8f40f1d9eb1fd8d/scipy/_lib/tests/test__util.py#L556

It also might be useful to see in how we handled some of the oddball cases, like `seed` arguments that already converted integers to `Generators` (instead of `RandomState`), existing `rng` arguments that worked more like most legacy `seed`/`random_state` arguments, and `random_state` attributes of classes, but I'm not sure if all that can be summarized.

allows `default_rng` to accept instances of `RandomState` and convert them to a `Generator`. The first stage of SciPy's SPEC 7 transition is essentially complete:

https://github.com/scipy/scipy/blob/82ee02556872b9f91576b835a8f40f1d9eb1fd8d/scipy/_lib/tests/test__util.py#L556

It also might be useful to see in how we handled some of the oddball cases, like `seed` arguments that already converted integers to `Generators` (instead of `RandomState`), existing `rng` arguments that worked more like most legacy `seed`/`random_state` arguments, and `random_state` attributes of classes, but I'm not sure if all that can be summarized.

:"DOC: 2.2.0","user":{"login":"mdhaber","id":6570539,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjY1NzA1Mzk=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/6570539?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber","html_url":"https://github.com/mdhaber","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mdhaber/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"numpy/numpy#27771 allows `default_rng` to accept instances of `RandomState` and convert them to a `Generator`. This updates the SPEC accordingly.

*Update: before merge, also correct single-backtick `Generator` in suggested `rng` documentation:*

> Types other than `numpy.random.Generator` are passed to `numpy.random.default_rng` to instantiate a `Generator`.

*This looks fine in markdown, but in ReST it needs two backticks.*

---

The first stage of SciPy's SPEC 7 transition is essentially complete:
https://github.com/scipy/scipy/issues/21833

For standard cases, other libraries might be interested in how we extended the decorator to inject standard documentation about the transition, e.g.

![{EB7EB439-218F-4660-8B30-EF4BDE09EB49}](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0d83245a-8cb2-46b8-9caf-9968b8f42ec6)

We also added standard tests:
https://github.com/scipy/scipy/blob/82ee02556872b9f91576b835a8f40f1d9eb1fd8d/scipy/_lib/tests/test__util.py#L556

It also might be useful to see in how we handled some of the oddball cases, like `seed` arguments that already converted integers to `Generators` (instead of `RandomState`), existing `rng` arguments that worked more like most legacy `seed`/`random_state` arguments, and `random_state` attributes of classes, but I'm not sure if all that can be summarized.

This updates the SPEC accordingly.

*Update: before merge, also correct single-backtick `Generator` in suggested `rng` documentation:*

> Types other than `numpy.random.Generator` are passed to `numpy.random.default_rng` to instantiate a `Generator`.

*This looks fine in markdown, but in ReST it needs two backticks.*

---

The first stage of SciPy's SPEC 7 transition is essentially complete:
https://github.com/scipy/scipy/issues/21833

For standard cases, other libraries might be interested in how we extended the decorator to inject standard documentation about the transition, e.g.

![{EB7EB439-218F-4660-8B30-EF4BDE09EB49}](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0d83245a-8cb2-46b8-9caf-9968b8f42ec6)

We also added standard tests:
https://github.com/scipy/scipy/blob/82ee02556872b9f91576b835a8f40f1d9eb1fd8d/scipy/_lib/tests/test__util.py#L556

It also might be useful to see in how we handled some of the oddball cases, like `seed` arguments that already converted integers to `Generators` (instead of `RandomState`), existing `rng` arguments that worked more like most legacy `seed`/`random_state` arguments, and `random_state` attributes of classes, but I'm not sure if all that can be summarized.

allows `default_rng` to accept instances of `RandomState` and convert them to a `Generator`. This updates the SPEC accordingly.

*Update: before merge, also correct single-backtick `Generator` in suggested `rng` documentation:*

> Types other than `numpy.random.Generator` are passed to `numpy.random.default_rng` to instantiate a `Generator`.

*This looks fine in markdown, but in ReST it needs two backticks.*

---

The first stage of SciPy's SPEC 7 transition is essentially complete:
https://github.com/scipy/scipy/issues/21833

For standard cases, other libraries might be interested in how we extended the decorator to inject standard documentation about the transition, e.g.

![{EB7EB439-218F-4660-8B30-EF4BDE09EB49}](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0d83245a-8cb2-46b8-9caf-9968b8f42ec6)

We also added standard tests:
https://github.com/scipy/scipy/blob/82ee02556872b9f91576b835a8f40f1d9eb1fd8d/scipy/_lib/tests/test__util.py#L556

It also might be useful to see in how we handled some of the oddball cases, like `seed` arguments that already converted integers to `Generators` (instead of `RandomState`), existing `rng` arguments that worked more like most legacy `seed`/`random_state` arguments, and `random_state` attributes of classes, but I'm not sure if all that can be summarized.

.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/pull/7566.patch","merged_at":"2024-12-05T16:34:04Z"},"body":"## Description

We have a rework of our footprint generating function on our [skimage2 agenda](https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/wiki/API-changes-for-skimage2#proposed-api-changes-doable-independent-of-skimage2). This WIP starts that work by combining "rectangular" footprint generators (`square`, `rectangle`, `cube`) into a single `skimage.morphology.footprint_rectangular` with ND support. 

This is also an opportunity to change to `decomposition="sequence"` as the default as suggested in the agenda. Though, I'm not sure if we really want to do this. It's arguably a more unexpected form to represent the footprint for the sake of performance. Usually, we decide that trade-off in favor of simpler behavior.

I plan to do the same for "elliptical" footprint generators in a separate PR to keep reviewing reasonable. 

I'm also playing around with a small wrapper class to help with the more complicated representation of decomposed footprints. I might move that into another PR depending on how this approach turns out.

## Checklist

<!-- Before pull requests can be merged, they should provide: -->

- A descriptive but concise pull request title
- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpydoc/blob/main/doc/example.py)
- [Unit tests](https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/development/contribute.html#testing)
- A gallery example in `./doc/examples` for new features
- [Contribution guide](https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/development/contribute.html) is followed

## Release note

For maintainers and optionally contributors, please refer to the [instructions](https://scikit-image.org/docs/stable/development/contribute.html#documenting-changes) on how to document this PR for the release notes.

Description

When the input arrays are of type `uint64`, adding a builtin type like `1` produces a `float64`. This change casts the result back to the input dtype.

Fixes #7291

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scikit-image/scikit-image/pulls/comments/1871797913","pull_request_review_id":2482403345,"id":1871797913,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAB6-0c5vkV6Z","diff_hunk":"@@ -1,68 +1,11 @@
 """Image Processing for Python
 
-``scikit-image`` (a.k.a. ``skimage``) is a collection of algorithms for image
+scikit-image (a.k.a. ``skimage``) is a collection of algorithms for image
 processing and computer vision.
 
 The main package of ``skimage`` only provides a few utilities for converting
-between image data types; for most features, you need to import one of the
-following subpackages:
-
-Subpackages
------------
-color
-    Color space conversion.
-data
-    Test images and example data.
-draw
-    Drawing primitives (lines, text, etc.) that operate on NumPy arrays.
-exposure
-    Image intensity adjustment, e.g., histogram equalization, etc.
-feature
-    Feature detection and extraction, e.g., texture analysis corners, etc.
-filters
-    Sharpening, edge finding, rank filters, thresholding, etc.
-graph
-    Graph-theoretic operations, e.g., shortest paths.
-io
-    Reading, saving, and displaying images and video.
-measure
-    Measurement of image properties, e.g., region properties and contours.
-metrics
-    Metrics corresponding to images, e.g. distance metrics, similarity, etc.
-morphology
-    Morphological operations, e.g., opening or skeletonization.
-restoration
-    Restoration algorithms, e.g., deconvolution algorithms, denoising, etc.
-segmentation
-    Partitioning an image into multiple regions.
-transform
-    Geometric and other transforms, e.g., rotation or the Radon transform.
-util

Description

Closes #6891
Closes #6904

Let me know if I should enable lazy loading for other submodules as well.

## Checklist

<!-- It's fine to submit PRs which are a work in progress! -->
<!-- But before they are merged, all PRs should provide: -->

- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/master/doc/example.py)
- Gallery example in `./doc/examples` (new features only)
- Benchmark in `./benchmarks`, if your changes aren't covered by an
  existing benchmark
- Unit tests
- Clean style in [the spirit of PEP8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/)
- Descriptive commit messages (see below)

<!-- For detailed information on these and other aspects see -->
<!-- the scikit-image contribution guidelines. -->
<!-- https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/contribute.html -->

## For reviewers

<!-- Don't remove the checklist below. -->

- Check that the PR title is short, concise, and will make sense 1 year
  later.
- Check that new functions are imported in corresponding `__init__.py`.
- Check that new features, API changes, and deprecations are mentioned in
  `doc/release/release_dev.rst`.
- There is a bot to help automate backporting a PR to an older branch. For
  example, to backport to v0.19.x after merging, add the following in a PR
  comment: `@meeseeksdev backport to v0.19.x`
- To run benchmarks on a PR, add the `run-benchmark` label. To rerun, the label
  can be removed and then added again. The benchmark output can be checked in
  the "Actions" tab.

Description

Closes #6891
Closes #6904

Let me know if I should enable lazy loading for other submodules as well.

## Checklist

<!-- It's fine to submit PRs which are a work in progress! -->
<!-- But before they are merged, all PRs should provide: -->

- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/master/doc/example.py)
- Gallery example in `./doc/examples` (new features only)
- Benchmark in `./benchmarks`, if your changes aren't covered by an
  existing benchmark
- Unit tests
- Clean style in [the spirit of PEP8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/)
- Descriptive commit messages (see below)

<!-- For detailed information on these and other aspects see -->
<!-- the scikit-image contribution guidelines. -->
<!-- https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/contribute.html -->

## For reviewers

<!-- Don't remove the checklist below. -->

- Check that the PR title is short, concise, and will make sense 1 year
  later.
- Check that new functions are imported in corresponding `__init__.py`.
- Check that new features, API changes, and deprecations are mentioned in
  `doc/release/release_dev.rst`.
- There is a bot to help automate backporting a PR to an older branch. For
  example, to backport to v0.19.x after merging, add the following in a PR
  comment: `@meeseeksdev backport to v0.19.x`
- To run benchmarks on a PR, add the `run-benchmark` label. To rerun, the label
  can be removed and then added again. The benchmark output can be checked in
  the "Actions" tab.

Description

Closes #6891
Closes #6904

Let me know if I should enable lazy loading for other submodules as well.

## Checklist

<!-- It's fine to submit PRs which are a work in progress! -->
<!-- But before they are merged, all PRs should provide: -->

- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/master/doc/example.py)
- Gallery example in `./doc/examples` (new features only)
- Benchmark in `./benchmarks`, if your changes aren't covered by an
  existing benchmark
- Unit tests
- Clean style in [the spirit of PEP8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/)
- Descriptive commit messages (see below)

<!-- For detailed information on these and other aspects see -->
<!-- the scikit-image contribution guidelines. -->
<!-- https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/contribute.html -->

## For reviewers

<!-- Don't remove the checklist below. -->

- Check that the PR title is short, concise, and will make sense 1 year
  later.
- Check that new functions are imported in corresponding `__init__.py`.
- Check that new features, API changes, and deprecations are mentioned in
  `doc/release/release_dev.rst`.
- There is a bot to help automate backporting a PR to an older branch. For
  example, to backport to v0.19.x after merging, add the following in a PR
  comment: `@meeseeksdev backport to v0.19.x`
- To run benchmarks on a PR, add the `run-benchmark` label. To rerun, the label
  can be removed and then added again. The benchmark output can be checked in
  the "Actions" tab.

Description

Closes #6891
Closes #6904

Let me know if I should enable lazy loading for other submodules as well.

## Checklist

<!-- It's fine to submit PRs which are a work in progress! -->
<!-- But before they are merged, all PRs should provide: -->

- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/master/doc/example.py)
- Gallery example in `./doc/examples` (new features only)
- Benchmark in `./benchmarks`, if your changes aren't covered by an
  existing benchmark
- Unit tests
- Clean style in [the spirit of PEP8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/)
- Descriptive commit messages (see below)

<!-- For detailed information on these and other aspects see -->
<!-- the scikit-image contribution guidelines. -->
<!-- https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/contribute.html -->

## For reviewers

<!-- Don't remove the checklist below. -->

- Check that the PR title is short, concise, and will make sense 1 year
  later.
- Check that new functions are imported in corresponding `__init__.py`.
- Check that new features, API changes, and deprecations are mentioned in
  `doc/release/release_dev.rst`.
- There is a bot to help automate backporting a PR to an older branch. For
  example, to backport to v0.19.x after merging, add the following in a PR
  comment: `@meeseeksdev backport to v0.19.x`
- To run benchmarks on a PR, add the `run-benchmark` label. To rerun, the label
  can be removed and then added again. The benchmark output can be checked in
  the "Actions" tab.

Description

Closes #6891
Closes #6904

Let me know if I should enable lazy loading for other submodules as well.

## Checklist

<!-- It's fine to submit PRs which are a work in progress! -->
<!-- But before they are merged, all PRs should provide: -->

- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/master/doc/example.py)
- Gallery example in `./doc/examples` (new features only)
- Benchmark in `./benchmarks`, if your changes aren't covered by an
  existing benchmark
- Unit tests
- Clean style in [the spirit of PEP8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/)
- Descriptive commit messages (see below)

<!-- For detailed information on these and other aspects see -->
<!-- the scikit-image contribution guidelines. -->
<!-- https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/contribute.html -->

## For reviewers

<!-- Don't remove the checklist below. -->

- Check that the PR title is short, concise, and will make sense 1 year
  later.
- Check that new functions are imported in corresponding `__init__.py`.
- Check that new features, API changes, and deprecations are mentioned in
  `doc/release/release_dev.rst`.
- There is a bot to help automate backporting a PR to an older branch. For
  example, to backport to v0.19.x after merging, add the following in a PR
  comment: `@meeseeksdev backport to v0.19.x`
- To run benchmarks on a PR, add the `run-benchmark` label. To rerun, the label
  can be removed and then added again. The benchmark output can be checked in
  the "Actions" tab.

Description

Closes #6891
Closes #6904

Let me know if I should enable lazy loading for other submodules as well.

## Checklist

<!-- It's fine to submit PRs which are a work in progress! -->
<!-- But before they are merged, all PRs should provide: -->

- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/master/doc/example.py)
- Gallery example in `./doc/examples` (new features only)
- Benchmark in `./benchmarks`, if your changes aren't covered by an
  existing benchmark
- Unit tests
- Clean style in [the spirit of PEP8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/)
- Descriptive commit messages (see below)

<!-- For detailed information on these and other aspects see -->
<!-- the scikit-image contribution guidelines. -->
<!-- https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/contribute.html -->

## For reviewers

<!-- Don't remove the checklist below. -->

- Check that the PR title is short, concise, and will make sense 1 year
  later.
- Check that new functions are imported in corresponding `__init__.py`.
- Check that new features, API changes, and deprecations are mentioned in
  `doc/release/release_dev.rst`.
- There is a bot to help automate backporting a PR to an older branch. For
  example, to backport to v0.19.x after merging, add the following in a PR
  comment: `@meeseeksdev backport to v0.19.x`
- To run benchmarks on a PR, add the `run-benchmark` label. To rerun, the label
  can be removed and then added again. The benchmark output can be checked in
  the "Actions" tab.

Description

Closes #6891
Closes #6904

Let me know if I should enable lazy loading for other submodules as well.

## Checklist

<!-- It's fine to submit PRs which are a work in progress! -->
<!-- But before they are merged, all PRs should provide: -->

- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/master/doc/example.py)
- Gallery example in `./doc/examples` (new features only)
- Benchmark in `./benchmarks`, if your changes aren't covered by an
  existing benchmark
- Unit tests
- Clean style in [the spirit of PEP8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/)
- Descriptive commit messages (see below)

<!-- For detailed information on these and other aspects see -->
<!-- the scikit-image contribution guidelines. -->
<!-- https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/contribute.html -->

## For reviewers

<!-- Don't remove the checklist below. -->

- Check that the PR title is short, concise, and will make sense 1 year
  later.
- Check that new functions are imported in corresponding `__init__.py`.
- Check that new features, API changes, and deprecations are mentioned in
  `doc/release/release_dev.rst`.
- There is a bot to help automate backporting a PR to an older branch. For
  example, to backport to v0.19.x after merging, add the following in a PR
  comment: `@meeseeksdev backport to v0.19.x`
- To run benchmarks on a PR, add the `run-benchmark` label. To rerun, the label
  can be removed and then added again. The benchmark output can be checked in
  the "Actions" tab.

imports","user":{"login":"lagru","id":20140352,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwMTQwMzUy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20140352?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/lagru","html_url":"https://github.com/lagru","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lagru/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lagru/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lagru/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lagru/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lagru/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lagru/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lagru/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lagru/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lagru/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-11-27T14:03:38Z","updated_at":"2024-12-05T17:48:42Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":true,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scientific-python/lazy-loader/pulls/133","html_url":"https://github.com/scientific-python/lazy-loader/pull/133","diff_url":"https://github.com/scientific-python/lazy-loader/pull/133.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/scientific-python/lazy-loader/pull/133.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"In scikit-image, I encountered the case where I want to make certain functions available for lazy-loading while at the same time not wanting to advertise their existence via `__all__` (see https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/pull/6892). To implement this with the current behavior of `lazy_loader.attach_stub` I had to specify `__all__` twice, both in the PY and in the PYI file (see https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/pull/6892/commits/40157d065dad032ac3068e4a3d5763580cd5f1d6). 

Ideally, I'd like to avoid this duplication. So I came up with a quick "work in progress" solution, in order to discuss this use case here. The current draft has some drawbacks for now, e.g. it makes certain assumptions about how `__all__` must be defined in the stub. I'm happy to iterate on this, depending on which kind of behavior is wanted.

Please, let me know what you think. :blush: ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scientific-python/lazy-loader/issues/133/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scientific-python/lazy-loader/issues/133/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scientific-python/lazy-loader/issues/comments/2521046463","html_url":"https://github.com/scientific-python/lazy-loader/pull/133#issuecomment-2521046463","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scientific-python/lazy-loader/issues/133","id":2521046463,"node_id":"IC_kwDOG6LUo86WRB2_","user":{"login":"stefanv","id":45071,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ1MDcx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/45071?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv","html_url":"https://github.com/stefanv","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-05T17:48:41Z","updated_at":"2024-12-05T17:48:41Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"`__all__` is really only used when doing `from X import *` isn't it? So, mostly I think `__dir__` is what we care about. Then there are two options: populate `__dir__` as everything that is lazy loaded, or take into consideration `__all__`.

Could that be a simpler approach, here, simply adding a `use_all` flag that changes both what comes back in `__all__` and in `__dir__`?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scientific-python/lazy-loader/issues/comments/2521046463/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

Description

Closes #6891
Closes #6904

Let me know if I should enable lazy loading for other submodules as well.

## Checklist

<!-- It's fine to submit PRs which are a work in progress! -->
<!-- But before they are merged, all PRs should provide: -->

- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/master/doc/example.py)
- Gallery example in `./doc/examples` (new features only)
- Benchmark in `./benchmarks`, if your changes aren't covered by an
  existing benchmark
- Unit tests
- Clean style in [the spirit of PEP8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/)
- Descriptive commit messages (see below)

<!-- For detailed information on these and other aspects see -->
<!-- the scikit-image contribution guidelines. -->
<!-- https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/contribute.html -->

## For reviewers

<!-- Don't remove the checklist below. -->

- Check that the PR title is short, concise, and will make sense 1 year
  later.
- Check that new functions are imported in corresponding `__init__.py`.
- Check that new features, API changes, and deprecations are mentioned in
  `doc/release/release_dev.rst`.
- There is a bot to help automate backporting a PR to an older branch. For
  example, to backport to v0.19.x after merging, add the following in a PR
  comment: `@meeseeksdev backport to v0.19.x`
- To run benchmarks on a PR, add the `run-benchmark` label. To rerun, the label
  can be removed and then added again. The benchmark output can be checked in
  the "Actions" tab.

Description

Closes #6891
Closes #6904

Let me know if I should enable lazy loading for other submodules as well.

## Checklist

<!-- It's fine to submit PRs which are a work in progress! -->
<!-- But before they are merged, all PRs should provide: -->

- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/master/doc/example.py)
- Gallery example in `./doc/examples` (new features only)
- Benchmark in `./benchmarks`, if your changes aren't covered by an
  existing benchmark
- Unit tests
- Clean style in [the spirit of PEP8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/)
- Descriptive commit messages (see below)

<!-- For detailed information on these and other aspects see -->
<!-- the scikit-image contribution guidelines. -->
<!-- https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/contribute.html -->

## For reviewers

<!-- Don't remove the checklist below. -->

- Check that the PR title is short, concise, and will make sense 1 year
  later.
- Check that new functions are imported in corresponding `__init__.py`.
- Check that new features, API changes, and deprecations are mentioned in
  `doc/release/release_dev.rst`.
- There is a bot to help automate backporting a PR to an older branch. For
  example, to backport to v0.19.x after merging, add the following in a PR
  comment: `@meeseeksdev backport to v0.19.x`
- To run benchmarks on a PR, add the `run-benchmark` label. To rerun, the label
  can be removed and then added again. The benchmark output can be checked in
  the "Actions" tab.

 """Image Processing for Python
 
-``scikit-image`` (a.k.a. ``skimage``) is a collection of algorithms for image
+scikit-image (a.k.a. ``skimage``) is a collection of algorithms for image
 processing and computer vision.
 
 The main package of ``skimage`` only provides a few utilities for converting
-between image data types; for most features, you need to import one of the
-following subpackages:
-
-Subpackages
------------
-color
-    Color space conversion.
-data
-    Test images and example data.
-draw
-    Drawing primitives (lines, text, etc.) that operate on NumPy arrays.
-exposure
-    Image intensity adjustment, e.g., histogram equalization, etc.
-feature
-    Feature detection and extraction, e.g., texture analysis corners, etc.
-filters
-    Sharpening, edge finding, rank filters, thresholding, etc.
-graph
-    Graph-theoretic operations, e.g., shortest paths.
-io
-    Reading, saving, and displaying images and video.
-measure
-    Measurement of image properties, e.g., region properties and contours.
-metrics
-    Metrics corresponding to images, e.g. distance metrics, similarity, etc.
-morphology
-    Morphological operations, e.g., opening or skeletonization.
-restoration
-    Restoration algorithms, e.g., deconvolution algorithms, denoising, etc.
-segmentation
-    Partitioning an image into multiple regions.
-transform
-    Geometric and other transforms, e.g., rotation or the Radon transform.
-util
-    Generic utilities.","path":"skimage/__init__.py","commit_id":"f4e3218205e4059dd67fb6142f69900d21bef856","original_commit_id":"85c15d70b472aa4fa17301f6ac0d9ebe3085f08a","user":{"login":"stefanv","id":45071,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ1MDcx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/45071?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv","html_url":"https://github.com/stefanv","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"The first one I checked was SciPy:

```
Subpackages
-----------
Using any of these subpackages requires an explicit import. For example,
``import scipy.cluster``.

::

 cluster                      --- Vector Quantization / Kmeans
 constants                    --- Physical and mathematical constants and units
 datasets                     --- Dataset methods
 fft                          --- Discrete Fourier transforms
 fftpack                      --- Legacy discrete Fourier transforms
 integrate                    --- Integration routines
 interpolate                  --- Interpolation Tools
 io                           --- Data input and output
 linalg                       --- Linear algebra routines
 misc                         --- Utilities that don't have another home.
 ndimage                      --- N-D image package
 odr                          --- Orthogonal Distance Regression
 optimize                     --- Optimization Tools
 signal                       --- Signal Processing Tools
 sparse                       --- Sparse Matrices
 spatial                      --- Spatial data structures and algorithms
 special                      --- Special functions
 stats                        --- Statistical Functions
```

For people interactively exploring at the prompt, there is no other way to concisely get a list of submodules + descriptions, Description

Closes #6891
Closes #6904

Let me know if I should enable lazy loading for other submodules as well.

## Checklist

<!-- It's fine to submit PRs which are a work in progress! -->
<!-- But before they are merged, all PRs should provide: -->

- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/master/doc/example.py)
- Gallery example in `./doc/examples` (new features only)
- Benchmark in `./benchmarks`, if your changes aren't covered by an
  existing benchmark
- Unit tests
- Clean style in [the spirit of PEP8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/)
- Descriptive commit messages (see below)

<!-- For detailed information on these and other aspects see -->
<!-- the scikit-image contribution guidelines. -->
<!-- https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/contribute.html -->

## For reviewers

<!-- Don't remove the checklist below. -->

- Check that the PR title is short, concise, and will make sense 1 year
  later.
- Check that new functions are imported in corresponding `__init__.py`.
- Check that new features, API changes, and deprecations are mentioned in
  `doc/release/release_dev.rst`.
- There is a bot to help automate backporting a PR to an older branch. For
  example, to backport to v0.19.x after merging, add the following in a PR
  comment: `@meeseeksdev backport to v0.19.x`
- To run benchmarks on a PR, add the `run-benchmark` label. To rerun, the label
  can be removed and then added again. The benchmark output can be checked in
  the "Actions" tab.

Description

Closes #6891
Closes #6904

Let me know if I should enable lazy loading for other submodules as well.

## Checklist

<!-- It's fine to submit PRs which are a work in progress! -->
<!-- But before they are merged, all PRs should provide: -->

- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/master/doc/example.py)
- Gallery example in `./doc/examples` (new features only)
- Benchmark in `./benchmarks`, if your changes aren't covered by an
  existing benchmark
- Unit tests
- Clean style in [the spirit of PEP8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/)
- Descriptive commit messages (see below)

<!-- For detailed information on these and other aspects see -->
<!-- the scikit-image contribution guidelines. -->
<!-- https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/contribute.html -->

## For reviewers

<!-- Don't remove the checklist below. -->

- Check that the PR title is short, concise, and will make sense 1 year
  later.
- Check that new functions are imported in corresponding `__init__.py`.
- Check that new features, API changes, and deprecations are mentioned in
  `doc/release/release_dev.rst`.
- There is a bot to help automate backporting a PR to an older branch. For
  example, to backport to v0.19.x after merging, add the following in a PR
  comment: `@meeseeksdev backport to v0.19.x`
- To run benchmarks on a PR, add the `run-benchmark` label. To rerun, the label
  can be removed and then added again. The benchmark output can be checked in
  the "Actions" tab.

 """Image Processing for Python
 
-``scikit-image`` (a.k.a. ``skimage``) is a collection of algorithms for image
+scikit-image (a.k.a. ``skimage``) is a collection of algorithms for image
 processing and computer vision.
 
 The main package of ``skimage`` only provides a few utilities for converting
-between image data types; for most features, you need to import one of the
-following subpackages:
-
-Subpackages
------------
-color
-    Color space conversion.
-data
-    Test images and example data.
-draw
-    Drawing primitives (lines, text, etc.) that operate on NumPy arrays.
-exposure
-    Image intensity adjustment, e.g., histogram equalization, etc.
-feature
-    Feature detection and extraction, e.g., texture analysis corners, etc.
-filters
-    Sharpening, edge finding, rank filters, thresholding, etc.
-graph
-    Graph-theoretic operations, e.g., shortest paths.
-io
-    Reading, saving, and displaying images and video.
-measure
-    Measurement of image properties, e.g., region properties and contours.
-metrics
-    Metrics corresponding to images, e.g. distance metrics, similarity, etc.
-morphology
-    Morphological operations, e.g., opening or skeletonization.
-restoration
-    Restoration algorithms, e.g., deconvolution algorithms, denoising, etc.
-segmentation
-    Partitioning an image into multiple regions.
-transform
-    Geometric and other transforms, e.g., rotation or the Radon transform.
-util
-    Generic Description

Closes #6891
Closes #6904

Let me know if I should enable lazy loading for other submodules as well.

## Checklist

<!-- It's fine to submit PRs which are a work in progress! -->
<!-- But before they are merged, all PRs should provide: -->

- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/master/doc/example.py)
- Gallery example in `./doc/examples` (new features only)
- Benchmark in `./benchmarks`, if your changes aren't covered by an
  existing benchmark
- Unit tests
- Clean style in [the spirit of PEP8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/)
- Descriptive commit messages (see below)

<!-- For detailed information on these and other aspects see -->
<!-- the scikit-image contribution guidelines. -->
<!-- https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/contribute.html -->

## For reviewers

<!-- Don't remove the checklist below. -->

- Check that the PR title is short, concise, and will make sense 1 year
  later.
- Check that new functions are imported in corresponding `__init__.py`.

Description

Closes #6891
Closes #6904

Let me know if I should enable lazy loading for other submodules as well.

## Checklist

<!-- It's fine to submit PRs which are a work in progress! -->
<!-- But before they are merged, all PRs should provide: -->

- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/master/doc/example.py)
- Gallery example in `./doc/examples` (new features only)
- Benchmark in `./benchmarks`, if your changes aren't covered by an
  existing benchmark
- Unit tests
- Clean style in [the spirit of PEP8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/)
- Descriptive commit messages (see below)

<!-- For detailed information on these and other aspects see -->
<!-- the scikit-image contribution guidelines. -->
<!-- https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/contribute.html -->

## For reviewers

<!-- Don't remove the checklist below. -->

- Check that the PR title is short, concise, and will make sense 1 year
  later.
- Check that new functions are imported in corresponding `__init__.py`.
- Check that new features, API changes, and deprecations are mentioned in
  `doc/release/release_dev.rst`.
- There is a bot to help automate backporting a PR to an older branch. For
  example, to backport to v0.19.x after merging, add the following in a PR
  comment: `@meeseeksdev backport to v0.19.x`
- To run benchmarks on a PR, add the `run-benchmark` label. To rerun, the label
  can be removed and then added again. The benchmark output can be checked in
  the "Actions" tab.

 """Image Processing for Python
 
-``scikit-image`` (a.k.a. ``skimage``) is a collection of algorithms for image
+scikit-image (a.k.a. ``skimage``) is a collection of algorithms for image
 processing and computer vision.
 
 The main package of ``skimage`` only provides a few utilities for converting
-between image data types; for most features, you need to import one of the
-following subpackages:
-
-Subpackages
------------
-color
-    Color space conversion.
-data
-    Test images and example data.
-draw
-    Drawing primitives (lines, text, etc.) that operate on NumPy arrays.
-exposure
-    Image intensity adjustment, e.g., histogram equalization, etc.
-feature
-    Feature detection and extraction, e.g., texture analysis corners, etc.
-filters
-    Sharpening, edge finding, rank filters, thresholding, etc.
-graph
-    Graph-theoretic operations, e.g., shortest paths.
-io
-    Reading, saving, and displaying images and video.
-measure
-    Measurement of image properties, e.g., region properties and contours.
-metrics
-    Metrics corresponding to images, e.g. distance metrics, similarity, etc.
-morphology
-    Morphological operations, e.g., opening or skeletonization.
-restoration
-    Restoration algorithms, e.g., deconvolution algorithms, denoising, etc.
-segmentation
-    Partitioning an image into multiple regions.
-transform
-    Geometric and other transforms, e.g., rotation or the Radon transform.
-util
-    Generic utilities.","path":"skimage/__init__.py","commit_id":"6d7a770d70c4c95b31761b6f44e014876ad13f0e","original_commit_id":"85c15d70b472aa4fa17301f6ac0d9ebe3085f08a","user":{"login":"stefanv","id":45071,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ1MDcx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/45071?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv","html_url":"https://github.com/stefanv","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stefanv/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"We could say "Image alignment algorithms" to make it more obvious what it does; but also fine as-is. Description

Closes #6891
Closes #6904

Let me know if I should enable lazy loading for other submodules as well.

## Checklist

<!-- It's fine to submit PRs which are a work in progress! -->
<!-- But before they are merged, all PRs should provide: -->

- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/master/doc/example.py)
- Gallery example in `./doc/examples` (new features only)
- Benchmark in `./benchmarks`, if your changes aren't covered by an
  existing benchmark
- Unit tests
- Clean style in [the spirit of PEP8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/)
- Descriptive commit messages (see below)

<!-- For detailed information on these and other aspects see -->
<!-- the scikit-image contribution guidelines. -->
<!-- https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/contribute.html -->

## For reviewers

<!-- Don't remove the checklist below. -->

- Check that the PR title is short, concise, and will make sense 1 year
  later.

- Check that new features, API changes, and deprecations are mentioned in

  comment: `@meeseeksdev backport to v0.19.x`

  the "Actions" tab.

Description

Closes #6891
Closes #6904

Let me know if I should enable lazy loading for other submodules as well.

## Checklist

<!-- It's fine to submit PRs which are a work in progress! -->
<!-- But before they are merged, all PRs should provide: -->

- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/master/doc/example.py)
- Gallery example in `./doc/examples` (new features only)
- Benchmark in `./benchmarks`, if your changes aren't covered by an
  existing benchmark
- Unit tests
- Clean style in [the spirit of PEP8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/)
- Descriptive commit messages (see below)

<!-- For detailed information on these and other aspects see -->
<!-- the scikit-image contribution guidelines. -->
<!-- https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/contribute.html -->

## For reviewers

<!-- Don't remove the checklist below. -->

- Check that the PR title is short, concise, and will make sense 1 year
  later.
- Check that new functions are imported in corresponding `__init__.py`.
- Check that new features, API changes, and deprecations are mentioned in
  `doc/release/release_dev.rst`.
- There is a bot to help automate backporting a PR to an older branch. For
  example, to backport to v0.19.x after merging, add the following in a PR

Description

Closes #6891
Closes #6904

Let me know if I should enable lazy loading for other submodules as well.

## Checklist

<!-- It's fine to submit PRs which are a work in progress! -->
<!-- But before they are merged, all PRs should provide: -->

- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/master/doc/example.py)
- Gallery example in `./doc/examples` (new features only)
- Benchmark in `./benchmarks`, if your changes aren't covered by an
  existing benchmark
- Unit tests
- Clean style in [the spirit of PEP8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/)
- Descriptive commit messages (see below)

<!-- For detailed information on these and other aspects see -->
<!-- the scikit-image contribution guidelines. -->
<!-- https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/contribute.html -->

## For reviewers

<!-- Don't remove the checklist below. -->

- Check that the PR title is short, concise, and will make sense 1 year
  later.
- Check that new functions are imported in corresponding `__init__.py`.
- Check that new features, API changes, and deprecations are mentioned in
  `doc/release/release_dev.rst`.
- There is a bot to help automate backporting a PR to an older branch. For
  example, to backport to v0.19.x after merging, add the following in a PR
  comment: `@meeseeksdev backport to v0.19.x`
- To run benchmarks on a PR, add the `run-benchmark` label. To rerun, the label
  can be removed and then added again. The benchmark output can be checked in
  the "Actions" tab.

Description

Closes #6891
Closes #6904

Let me know if I should enable lazy loading for other submodules as well.

## Checklist

<!-- It's fine to submit PRs which are a work in progress! -->
<!-- But before they are merged, all PRs should provide: -->

- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/master/doc/example.py)
- Gallery example in `./doc/examples` (new features only)
- Benchmark in `./benchmarks`, if your changes aren't covered by an
  existing benchmark
- Unit tests
- Clean style in [the spirit of PEP8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/)
- Descriptive commit messages (see below)

<!-- For detailed information on these and other aspects see -->
<!-- the scikit-image contribution guidelines. -->
<!-- https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/contribute.html -->

## For reviewers

<!-- Don't remove the checklist below. -->

- Check that the PR title is short, concise, and will make sense 1 year
  later.
- Check that new functions are imported in corresponding `__init__.py`.
- Check that new features, API changes, and deprecations are mentioned in
  `doc/release/release_dev.rst`.
- There is a bot to help automate backporting a PR to an older branch. For
  example, to backport to v0.19.x after merging, add the following in a PR
  comment: `@meeseeksdev backport to v0.19.x`
- To run benchmarks on a PR, add the `run-benchmark` label. To rerun, the label
  can be removed and then added again. The benchmark output can be checked in

Description

Closes #6891
Closes #6904

Let me know if I should enable lazy loading for other submodules as well.

## Checklist

<!-- It's fine to submit PRs which are a work in progress! -->
<!-- But before they are merged, all PRs should provide: -->

- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/master/doc/example.py)
- Gallery example in `./doc/examples` (new features only)
- Benchmark in `./benchmarks`, if your changes aren't covered by an
  existing benchmark
- Unit tests
- Clean style in [the spirit of PEP8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/)
- Descriptive commit messages (see below)

<!-- For detailed information on these and other aspects see -->
<!-- the scikit-image contribution guidelines. -->
<!-- https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/contribute.html -->

## For reviewers

<!-- Don't remove the checklist below. -->

- Check that the PR title is short, concise, and will make sense 1 year
  later.
- Check that new functions are imported in corresponding `__init__.py`.
- Check that new features, API changes, and deprecations are mentioned in
  `doc/release/release_dev.rst`.
- There is a bot to help automate backporting a PR to an older branch. For
  example, to backport to v0.19.x after merging, add the following in a PR
  comment: `@meeseeksdev backport to v0.19.x`
- To run benchmarks on a PR, add the `run-benchmark` label. To rerun, the label
  can be removed and then added again. The benchmark output can be checked in
  the "Actions" tab.

Description

Closes #6891
Closes #6904

Let me know if I should enable lazy loading for other submodules as well.

## Checklist

<!-- It's fine to submit PRs which are a work in progress! -->
<!-- But before they are merged, all PRs should provide: -->

- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/master/doc/example.py)
- Gallery example in `./doc/examples` (new features only)
- Benchmark in `./benchmarks`, if your changes aren't covered by an
  existing benchmark
- Unit tests
- Clean style in [the spirit of PEP8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/)
- Descriptive commit messages (see below)

<!-- For detailed information on these and other aspects see -->
<!-- the scikit-image contribution guidelines. -->
<!-- https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/contribute.html -->

## For reviewers

<!-- Don't remove the checklist below. -->

- Check that the PR title is short, concise, and will make sense 1 year
  later.
- Check that new functions are imported in corresponding `__init__.py`.
- Check that new features, API changes, and deprecations are mentioned in
  `doc/release/release_dev.rst`.
- There is a bot to help automate backporting a PR to an older branch. For
  example, to backport to v0.19.x after merging, add the following in a PR
  comment: `@meeseeksdev backport to v0.19.x`
- To run benchmarks on a PR, add the `run-benchmark` label. To rerun, the label
  can be removed and then added again. The benchmark output can be checked in
  the "Actions" tab.

Description

Closes #6891
Closes #6904

Let me know if I should enable lazy loading for other submodules as well.

## Checklist

<!-- It's fine to submit PRs which are a work in progress! -->
<!-- But before they are merged, all PRs should provide: -->

- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/master/doc/example.py)
- Gallery example in `./doc/examples` (new features only)
- Benchmark in `./benchmarks`, if your changes aren't covered by an
  existing benchmark
- Unit tests
- Clean style in [the spirit of PEP8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/)
- Descriptive commit messages (see below)

<!-- For detailed information on these and other aspects see -->
<!-- the scikit-image contribution guidelines. -->
<!-- https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/contribute.html -->

## For reviewers

<!-- Don't remove the checklist below. -->

- Check that the PR title is short, concise, and will make sense 1 year
  later.
- Check that new functions are imported in corresponding `__init__.py`.
- Check that new features, API changes, and deprecations are mentioned in
  `doc/release/release_dev.rst`.
- There is a bot to help automate backporting a PR to an older branch. For
  example, to backport to v0.19.x after merging, add the following in a PR
  comment: `@meeseeksdev backport to v0.19.x`
- To run benchmarks on a PR, add the `run-benchmark` label. To rerun, the label
  can be removed and then added again. The benchmark output can be checked in
  the "Actions" tab.

The following code seems correct, and passes type checking under 2.1.3 but not 2.2.0:

```python
import numpy as np

rng = np.random.default_rng(27446968)
n_states = 5

P = rng.random(size=(n_states, n_states))
p = rng.random(n_states)

p = P.T @ p
```

I suppose mypy is flagging that the float width can increase, but since float64 is used by random, that won't happen in this scenario.

### Reproduce the code example:

```python
N/A
```

Found 1 error in 1 file (checked 1 source file)
```


### Python and NumPy Versions:

Python 3.13, mypy 1.13, numpy 2.2.0

Description

Closes #6891
Closes #6904

Let me know if I should enable lazy loading for other submodules as well.

## Checklist

<!-- It's fine to submit PRs which are a work in progress! -->
<!-- But before they are merged, all PRs should provide: -->

- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/master/doc/example.py)
- Gallery example in `./doc/examples` (new features only)
- Benchmark in `./benchmarks`, if your changes aren't covered by an
  existing benchmark
- Unit tests
- Clean style in [the spirit of PEP8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/)
- Descriptive commit messages (see below)

<!-- For detailed information on these and other aspects see -->
<!-- the scikit-image contribution guidelines. -->
<!-- https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/contribute.html -->

## For reviewers

<!-- Don't remove the checklist below. -->

- Check that the PR title is short, concise, and will make sense 1 year
  later.
- Check that new functions are imported in corresponding `__init__.py`.
- Check that new features, API changes, and deprecations are mentioned in
  `doc/release/release_dev.rst`.
- There is a bot to help automate backporting a PR to an older branch. For
  example, to backport to v0.19.x after merging, add the following in a PR
  comment: `@meeseeksdev backport to v0.19.x`
- To run benchmarks on a PR, add the `run-benchmark` label. To rerun, the label
  can be removed and then added again. The benchmark output can be checked in
  the "Actions" tab.

Description

Closes #6891
Closes #6904

Let me know if I should enable lazy loading for other submodules as well.

## Checklist

<!-- It's fine to submit PRs which are a work in progress! -->
<!-- But before they are merged, all PRs should provide: -->

- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/master/doc/example.py)
- Gallery example in `./doc/examples` (new features only)
- Benchmark in `./benchmarks`, if your changes aren't covered by an
  existing benchmark
- Unit tests
- Clean style in [the spirit of PEP8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/)
- Descriptive commit messages (see below)

<!-- For detailed information on these and other aspects see -->
<!-- the scikit-image contribution guidelines. -->
<!-- https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/contribute.html -->

## For reviewers

<!-- Don't remove the checklist below. -->

- Check that the PR title is short, concise, and will make sense 1 year
  later.
- Check that new functions are imported in corresponding `__init__.py`.
- Check that new features, API changes, and deprecations are mentioned in
  `doc/release/release_dev.rst`.
- There is a bot to help automate backporting a PR to an older branch. For
  example, to backport to v0.19.x after merging, add the following in a PR
  comment: `@meeseeksdev backport to v0.19.x`
- To run benchmarks on a PR, add the `run-benchmark` label. To rerun, the label
  can be removed and then added again. The benchmark output can be checked in
  the "Actions" tab.

Description

Closes #6891
Closes #6904

Let me know if I should enable lazy loading for other submodules as well.

## Checklist

<!-- It's fine to submit PRs which are a work in progress! -->
<!-- But before they are merged, all PRs should provide: -->

- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/master/doc/example.py)
- Gallery example in `./doc/examples` (new features only)
- Benchmark in `./benchmarks`, if your changes aren't covered by an
  existing benchmark
- Unit tests
- Clean style in [the spirit of PEP8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/)
- Descriptive commit messages (see below)

<!-- For detailed information on these and other aspects see -->
<!-- the scikit-image contribution guidelines. -->
<!-- https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/contribute.html -->

## For reviewers

<!-- Don't remove the checklist below. -->

- Check that the PR title is short, concise, and will make sense 1 year
  later.
- Check that new functions are imported in corresponding `__init__.py`.
- Check that new features, API changes, and deprecations are mentioned in
  `doc/release/release_dev.rst`.
- There is a bot to help automate backporting a PR to an older branch. For
  example, to backport to v0.19.x after merging, add the following in a PR
  comment: `@meeseeksdev backport to v0.19.x`
- To run benchmarks on a PR, add the `run-benchmark` label. To rerun, the label
  can be removed and then added again. The benchmark output can be checked in
  the "Actions" tab.

Description

Closes #6891
Closes #6904

Let me know if I should enable lazy loading for other submodules as well.

## Checklist

<!-- It's fine to submit PRs which are a work in progress! -->
<!-- But before they are merged, all PRs should provide: -->

- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/master/doc/example.py)
- Gallery example in `./doc/examples` (new features only)
- Benchmark in `./benchmarks`, if your changes aren't covered by an
  existing benchmark
- Unit tests
- Clean style in [the spirit of PEP8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/)
- Descriptive commit messages (see below)

<!-- For detailed information on these and other aspects see -->
<!-- the scikit-image contribution guidelines. -->
<!-- https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/contribute.html -->

## For reviewers

<!-- Don't remove the checklist below. -->

- Check that the PR title is short, concise, and will make sense 1 year
  later.
- Check that new functions are imported in corresponding `__init__.py`.
- Check that new features, API changes, and deprecations are mentioned in
  `doc/release/release_dev.rst`.
- There is a bot to help automate backporting a PR to an older branch. For
  example, to backport to v0.19.x after merging, add the following in a PR
  comment: `@meeseeksdev backport to v0.19.x`
- To run benchmarks on a PR, add the `run-benchmark` label. To rerun, the label
  can be removed and then added again. The benchmark output can be checked in
  the "Actions" tab.

Closes #6891
Closes #6904

Let me know if I should enable lazy loading for other submodules as well.

## Checklist

<!-- It's fine to submit PRs which are a work in progress! -->
<!-- But before they are merged, all PRs should provide: -->

- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/master/doc/example.py)
- Gallery example in `./doc/examples` (new features only)
- Benchmark in `./benchmarks`, if your changes aren't covered by an
  existing benchmark
- Unit tests
- Clean style in [the spirit of PEP8](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/)
- Descriptive commit messages (see below)

<!-- For detailed information on these and other aspects see -->
<!-- the scikit-image contribution guidelines. -->
<!-- https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/contribute.html -->

## For reviewers

<!-- Don't remove the checklist below. -->

- Check that the PR title is short, concise, and will make sense 1 year
  later.
- Check that new functions are imported in corresponding `__init__.py`.
- Check that new features, API changes, and deprecations are mentioned in
  `doc/release/release_dev.rst`.
- There is a bot to help automate backporting a PR to an older branch. For
  example, to backport to v0.19.x after merging, add the following in a PR
  comment: `@meeseeksdev backport to v0.19.x`
- To run benchmarks on a PR, add the `run-benchmark` label. To rerun, the label
  can be removed and then added again. The benchmark output can be checked in
  the "Actions" tab.

Bug","color":"e31c3d","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2023-04-21T07:08:25Z","updated_at":"2024-12-10T16:00:35Z","closed_at":"2024-12-10T16:00:35Z","author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Description:

Even though [`skimage/__init__.pyi` defines `__all__`](https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/blob/f03a55fe4b63ae494586cfbe39143528902747b0/skimage/__init__.pyi#L21) it is not available at runtime. In this line 

https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/blob/f03a55fe4b63ae494586cfbe39143528902747b0/skimage/__init__.py#L74

`attach_stub`'s third return value is the content of `__all__` from the PYI file but it is ignored. It seems intentional but I doubt that it's a good thing to leave `__all__` undefined for our `skimage` top-level namespace.

As far as I'm aware the only [import related effect of `__all__` is on the result of  `from skimage import *`](https://docs.python.org/3/tutorial/modules.html?highlight=__all__#importing-from-a-package). While that use case may be discouraged, I also tend to rely on the `__all__` variable to see what's intended to be part of our public API. I think this assumption may be also common to a lot of users and is also taken by tools such as [Sphinx's autosummary extension](https://www.sphinx-doc.org/en/master/usage/extensions/autosummary.html#confval-autosummary_ignore_module_all).

I suggest to update the above mentioned line to 
```python
__getattr__, __lazy_dir__, __all__ = lazy.attach_stub(__name__, __file__) 
```

Context: I noticed this while looking into a way to crawl our public API.

Related #6892.

### Way to reproduce:

```python
import skimage
skimage.__all__  
# AttributeError: No skimage attribute __all__
```

### Version information:

```Shell
3.11.0 | packaged by conda-forge | (main, Jan 14 2023, 12:27:40) [GCC 11.3.0]
Linux-6.2.11-arch1-1-x86_64-with-glibc2.37
scikit-image version: 0.21.0rc1.dev0+git20230420.f03a55fe4
numpy version: 1.24.2
```

{"repository_id":2014929,"push_id":21638056496,"size":1,"distinct_size":1,"ref":"refs/heads/main","head":"a2a80b7387147a8b12131cac851a0fc102c65d6a","before":"aa7042ccd227e7f49b73ac466542088ec5a9ad63","commits":[{"sha":"a2a80b7387147a8b12131cac851a0fc102c65d6a","author":{"email":"3f8e01313814a03a9f317913ab169678ef0a55e5@mailbox.org","name":"Lars Grüter"},"message":"Lazy load legacy imports in `skimage` top module (#6892)

* Lazy load legacy imports in skimage top module

* Add convenience functions to __all__

These functions are advertised in skimage's docstring. I don't really
see a reason why `data_dir` must be public in the root namespace. So
keep it hidden.

* Derive __all__ form lazy stub loader

Curiously, this still doesn't include __version__ in skimage.__all__.
Maybe, this is a bug or behavior in lazy_loader that should be updated?

* Reorder doctest result

I'm not sure why the order changed. Likely changing the import behavior
to use lazy loading fully at the top-level of `skimage`, changes the
order in which candidates are found by `lookfor` as well.

Try if this addresses all test failures.

* Don't use __all__ provided by lazy_loader.attach_stub

attach_stub will include every import it finds in the PYI file. However,
we no longer want to advertise utility functions in the top-level
namespace. This seems to be a workaround, that keeps things like
`skimage.img_as_float` usable at runtime, but doesn't expose them via
`__all__` or `__dir__`.

Additionally, this avoids listing these utility functions twice in
different places of our HTML documentation with Sphinx.

* Revert "Reorder doctest result"

* Add explanatory comment about duplicating __all__

If .py and .pyi file were to define different __all__ (by using the one
returned by `attach_stub`), different tools might behave inconsistent,
depending on which file they prefer. Let's avoid that by being explicit.

Let's raise this issue upstream with lazy_loader too.

* Merge subpackage descriptions, use single source of truth

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Marianne Corvellec <marianne.corvellec@ens-lyon.org>

* Keep ski.__dir__ consistent with ski.__getattr__

The objects available via both functions should be consistent.
Approach inspired by https://discuss.python.org/t/4202/.

* Simplify adding commit info to dev version

Not sure if the previous approach would ever terminate the opened
process. I feel like using a dedicated function and `check_output`
simplifies things a lot and also keeps the global namespace cleaner.

* Make _PytestTester private from the start

It seems easier to import PytestTester as a private symbol from the
start instead of deleting it manually.

* Address feedback from code review

Co-authored-by: Stefan van der Walt <stefanv@berkeley.edu>
Co-authored-by: Marianne Corvellec <marianne.corvellec@ens-lyon.org>

* Remove outdated __SKIMAGE_SETUP__ flag in top-level __init__.py

This flag used to be set in setup.py, which is no longer the case. So
it should be safe to remove.

Co-authored-by: Stefan van der Walt <stefanv@berkeley.edu>

* Add subpackage description for registration

* Re-add explicit list of subpackages to skimage docstring

There are no other good ways to get a list of subpackags and their
descriptions locally.

Also make sure that the newly documented attribute `__version__` isn't
confused up by the documentation tooling with the actual line where
`__version__` is defined.

* Remove outdated paragraph on top-level vs subpackages

* Exclude builtin private attributes in ski.__dir__

There's no consensus on whether builtin private attributes like
`__file__`, etc. should be returned by __dir__. So keep the status quo
in that regard but still hide former utility functions we no longer want
to advertise.

* Make __dir__ return a copy of __all__

---------

Co-authored-by: Marianne Corvellec <marianne.corvellec@ens-lyon.org>
Co-authored-by: Stefan van der Walt <stefanv@berkeley.edu>","distinct":true,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scikit-image/scikit-image/commits/a2a80b7387147a8b12131cac851a0fc102c65d6a"}]}

running tests on Python 3.13, a KeyError occurs in the skimage.measure._regionprops module during the installation of property documentation via _install_properties_docs(). The error is specifically triggered by an attempt to assign a documentation string to a non-existent or mismatched property, area_bbox, in the RegionProperties class.

The root cause appears to be that `__debug__` is enabled by default when running tests. This leads to the installation of the documentation even when `__debug__` is used to determine test-related behavior. The code might rely on the assumption that certain properties or keys exist, but they are either renamed or not present in this particular Python version or skimage release.

The error occurs in the following code: ([link](https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/blob/1eec6600128e8db85531406d5c5cb00e3d2c61d3/skimage/measure/_regionprops.py#L1435))
```python3
getattr(RegionProperties, p).__doc__ = prop_doc[p]
```

Might be related to: https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/pull/7463/files#r1678135375

The error:

```python3
/usr/local/lib/python3.13/site-packages/skimage/measure/_regionprops.py:1426: in <module>
    _install_properties_docs()
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _

    def _install_properties_docs():
        prop_doc = _parse_docs()
    
        for p in [member for member in dir(RegionProperties) if not member.startswith('_')]:
>           getattr(RegionProperties, p).__doc__ = prop_doc[p]
E           KeyError: 'area_bbox'

/usr/local/lib/python3.13/site-packages/skimage/measure/_regionprops.py:1421: KeyError
```

Also I'm trying to patching it but is not working...

```python3
from pytest import fixture
from unittest.mock import patch

@fixture(autouse=True)
def patch_install_properties_docs():
    print("Patching _install_properties_docs")
    with patch('skimage.measure._regionprops._install_properties_docs', lambda: None):
        yield
    print("Unpatching _install_properties_docs")
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scikit-image/scikit-image/issues/7585/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scikit-image/scikit-image/issues/7585/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"}}

Description

The problem here is that the [old API of `octagon`](https://scikit-image.org/docs/stable/api/skimage.morphology.html#skimage.morphology.octagon) and its implementation doesn't map easily to an API using `shape` that allows for varying dimension lengths. If no decomposition is used, I've almost figured it out...

Let's discuss it at the meeting.

## Checklist

- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpydoc/blob/main/doc/example.py)

- A gallery example in `./doc/examples` for new features
- [Contribution guide](https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/development/contribute.html) is followed

## Release note

For maintainers and optionally contributors, please refer to the [instructions](https://scikit-image.org/docs/stable/development/contribute.html#documenting-changes) on how to document this PR for the release notes.

Description

The problem here is that the [old API of `octagon`](https://scikit-image.org/docs/stable/api/skimage.morphology.html#skimage.morphology.octagon) and its implementation doesn't map easily to an API using `shape` that allows for varying dimension lengths. If no decomposition is used, I've almost figured it out...

Let's discuss it at the meeting.

## Checklist

- [Docstrings for all functions](https://github.com/numpy/numpydoc/blob/main/doc/example.py)

- A gallery example in `./doc/examples` for new features
- [Contribution guide](https://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/development/contribute.html) is followed

Describe the issue:

The following code seems correct, and passes type checking under 2.1.3 but not 2.2.0:

```python
import numpy as np

rng = np.random.default_rng(27446968)
n_states = 5

P = rng.random(size=(n_states, n_states))
p = rng.random(n_states)

p = P.T @ p
```

I suppose mypy is flagging that the float width can increase, but since float64 is used by random, that won't happen in this scenario.

### Reproduce the code example:

```python
N/A
```


### Error message:

```shell
foo.py:10: error: Incompatible types in assignment (expression has type "ndarray[tuple[int, ...], dtype[floating[Any]]]", variable has type "ndarray[tuple[int, ...], dtype[float64]]")  [assignment]
Found 1 error in 1 file (checked 1 source file)
```


### Python and NumPy Versions:

Python 3.13, mypy 1.13, numpy 2.2.0

In this case, the only way to prevent this, is using shape-typing.

I thought I am assigning a shape `(5,)` array to a shape `(5,)` array, but perhaps I missed some subtlety?

```
P.shape=(5, 5) p.shape=(5,)
(P.T @ p).shape=(5,)
```

From above, the error message:

```
foo.py:9: error: Incompatible types in assignment (expression has type "ndarray[tuple[int, ...], dtype[floating[Any]]]", variable has type "ndarray[tuple[int, ...], dtype[float64]]")  [assignment]
```

Describe the issue:

The following code seems correct, and passes type checking under 2.1.3 but not 2.2.0:

```python
import numpy as np

rng = np.random.default_rng(27446968)
n_states = 5

P = rng.random(size=(n_states, n_states))
p = rng.random(n_states)

p = P.T @ p
```

I suppose mypy is flagging that the float width can increase, but since float64 is used by random, that won't happen in this scenario.

### Reproduce the code example:

```python
N/A
```


### Error message:

```shell
foo.py:10: error: Incompatible types in assignment (expression has type "ndarray[tuple[int, ...], dtype[floating[Any]]]", variable has type "ndarray[tuple[int, ...], dtype[float64]]")  [assignment]
Found 1 error in 1 file (checked 1 source file)
```


### Python and NumPy Versions:

Python 3.13, mypy 1.13, numpy 2.2.0

This looked promising, but it's very strict. E.g., I simplified my example above. In the lecture notes it is:

```python
# Normalize p
p /= p.sum()

# Take steps
for k in range(n_steps):
    p = P.T @ p
```

That for-loop, because it is at a different block/nesting depth, still fails the check even with `--allow-redefinition`.

Problem

From `plot_bar.py` in the SP lecture notes, we have:

```python
X = np.arange(n)
rng = np.random.default_rng()
Y1 = (1 - X / float(n)) * rng.uniform(0.5, 1.0, n)

for x, y in zip(X, Y1):
    plt.text(x, y + 0.05, f"{y:.2f}", ha="center", va="bottom")
```

Mypy reports typing errors:

```
intro/matplotlib/examples/plot_bar.py:22: error: Argument 1 to "text" has incompatible type "signedinteger[Any]"; expected "float"  [arg-type]
intro/matplotlib/examples/plot_bar.py:22: error: Argument 2 to "text" has incompatible type "floating[Any] | float64"; expected "float"  [arg-type]
intro/matplotlib/examples/plot_bar.py:25: error: Argument 1 to "text" has incompatible type "signedinteger[Any]"; expected "float"  [arg-type]
intro/matplotlib/examples/plot_bar.py:25: error: Argument 2 to "text" has incompatible type "floating[Any] | float64"; expected "float"  [arg-type]
```

### Proposed solution


Could we broaden the floating point types accepted?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/matplotlib/matplotlib/issues/29332/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/matplotlib/matplotlib/issues/29332/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}
